title: Johnson & Johnson — Overview
html:
Module 1: J&J Account Overview

Governing Thought

J&J Innovative Medicine is ZoomRx's largest and most complex account — spanning 6 therapeutic areas, 15+ brands, and 25+ active project types across a partnership that dates back to 2014. The account's complexity stems not from any single brand but from the sheer breadth of concurrent workstreams: a Principal must hold context across oncology (lung, GU, heme), immunology, neuroscience, and cardiopulmonary simultaneously, with each TA running its own mix of sales force tracking, congress analytics, MSL monitoring, and commercial research. Mastering the brand portfolio, competitive dynamics, and ZoomRx service matrix is table stakes for managing this relationship.

The J&J Landscape: What You Need to Know

J&J Innovative Medicine at a Glance

J&J Innovative Medicine (formerly Janssen) is the pharmaceutical arm of Johnson & Johnson, restructured after the Kenvue consumer health spin-off. The division operates across six therapeutic areas relevant to our work:

Dimension Detail
Therapeutic Areas Oncology (Lung, GU, Heme), Immunology, Neuroscience, Cardiopulmonary
Brands we support 15+ (RYBREVANT, ERLEADA, DARZALEX, TECVAYLI, TALVEY, AKEEGA, INLEXZO, SPRAVATO, TREMFYA, and others)
Partnership tenure Since 2014 (10+ years)
Active project types SFEA, Ferma Congress, OCE Tracking, MSL Tracking, FRM Tracking, KAM Tracking, ATU, Launch KPI, Digital Tracking, Patient Experience, Qual Interviews, Sales Simulation, and more

Why it matters: No other ZoomRx account touches this many TAs and project types simultaneously. Expect overlapping timelines, shared stakeholders across brands, and constant pipeline-driven scope expansion.

Oncology Portfolio (Primary Revenue Driver)

Oncology generates the largest share of J&J project volume across three sub-TAs: Lung, GU (prostate/bladder), and Hematology.

RYBREVANT (amivantamab) — Lung/NSCLC

  • What it is: Bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and MET; J&J's flagship lung oncology asset
  • Indications: 2L+ exon 20 EGFR NSCLC (mono), 1L exon 20 + chemo, 1L common EGFR mutations + lazertinib (LAZCLUZE), 2L common EGFR + chemo
  • Market size: ~30,000 eligible US patients/year with EGFR-mutated NSCLC
  • Key competitor: TAGRISSO (osimertinib, AstraZeneca) — the incumbent standard of care
  • Pivotal data: MARIPOSA trial showed PFS 23.7 vs 16.6 months versus osimertinib in 1L common EGFR; other key trials include CHRYSALIS, PAPILLON, MARIPOSA-2, COCOON
  • Pipeline risk: SubQ formulation delayed (CRL); expansion into Head & Neck and Colorectal/GI (OrigAMI trial)
  • Brand AOR: DDB Health
  • ZoomRx work: Sales Rep Tracking (SFEA), OCE Tracking, MSL Tracking, FRM Tracking, KAM Tracking, Ferma Congress

Principal note: RYBREVANT is our highest-touch brand. The SFEA alone runs monthly waves with multi-slide readouts. The MARIPOSA vs TAGRISSO competitive dynamic drives most of the analytical narrative.

ERLEADA (apalutamide) — Prostate/GU

  • What it is: Next-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI)
  • Indications: nmCRPC, mCSPC (+ ADT)
  • Market size: 300,000+ new prostate cancer cases/year; nmCRPC ~13-60K/year, mCSPC ~18-45K/year
  • Key competitors: Xtandi (enzalutamide, Pfizer/Astellas), Nubeqa (darolutamide, Bayer)
  • Pivotal data: SPARTAN (MFS 40.5 vs 16.2 mo in nmCRPC), TITAN (OS HR 0.65 in mCSPC)
  • Ongoing trials: ATLAS, PROTEUS, ARANOTE
  • ZoomRx work: Sales Rep Tracking (SFEA), MSL Tracking, FRM Tracking, KAM Tracking, ATU, Ferma Congress

AKEEGA (niraparib + abiraterone) — Prostate/GU

  • What it is: First-in-class combination of a PARP inhibitor and AR inhibitor for BRCA1/2-positive mCRPC
  • Key trial: MAGNITUDE (rPFS 16.5 vs 10.9 mo in BRCA+ population)
  • Critical nuance: No benefit in HRR-negative patients — this is a biomarker-driven label, which shapes messaging and rep conversations
  • Ongoing: AMPLITUDE trial in mCSPC
  • ZoomRx work: Covered under GU umbrella workstreams

INLEXZO (gemcitabine intravesical / TAR-200) — Bladder/GU

  • What it is: Sustained-release intravesical delivery system for BCG-unresponsive NMIBC with CIS
  • Approved: September 2025. CR rate 82% at 3 months, >50% maintained at 12+ months
  • Market size: 1,700-4,900 patients/year (approved indication) — small but high unmet need
  • Key competitors: Adstiladrin, Anktiva
  • Pipeline: SunRISe-3 (MIBC, bladder-sparing), SunRISe-4 (BCG-naive), SunRISe-5 (recurrent) — significant expansion potential
  • ZoomRx work: Launch KPI Tracking likely; watch for new project requests as launch matures

Hematology Brands — DARZALEX, TECVAYLI, TALVEY

  • DARZALEX (daratumumab): Anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody; backbone of multiple myeloma treatment across lines. Mature brand with established market leadership. ZoomRx runs Commercial Analytics, SFEA, Launch KPI, OCE, FRM, KAM, Digital Tracking, Patient ATU, Patient Experience, and Bispecifics Pharmacists Tracking
  • TECVAYLI (teclistamab): BCMAxCD3 bispecific for relapsed/refractory MM. Part of J&J's bispecific franchise push
  • TALVEY (talquetamab): GPRC5DxCD3 bispecific for relapsed/refractory MM. Differentiated MOA (non-BCMA target)
  • ZoomRx work: Heme has the broadest project mix of any sub-TA — 13+ active project types including Qual Interviews and Sales Simulation

Principal note: Heme is the deepest single sub-TA engagement. If you inherit this, expect the most complex stakeholder map on the account.

Immunology, Neuroscience & Cardiopulmonary

These TAs generate meaningful but lower-volume work compared to oncology. They tend to run fewer concurrent project types but with consistent cadence.

TREMFYA (guselkumab) — Immunology

  • What it is: IL-23 inhibitor across Gastroenterology, Dermatology, and Rheumatology
  • ZoomRx work: Ferma Congress, KAM & ABS Tracking, FRM Tracking, MSL Tracking
  • Why it matters: Spans three sub-TAs within immunology, meaning three separate client teams with overlapping but distinct needs

SPRAVATO (esketamine) — Neuroscience

  • What it is: Nasal esketamine for treatment-resistant depression (TRD) and major depressive disorder with suicidal ideation
  • ZoomRx work: Sales Rep Tracking (SFEA), HCP-Patient Conversations, MSL Tracking
  • Why it matters: Unique project type (HCP-Patient Conversations) not seen in other TAs; REMS program creates distinct access dynamics worth understanding

Cardiopulmonary

  • ZoomRx work: Sales Rep Tracking (SFEA), Commercial Analytics, Message Testing
  • Why it matters: Smaller engagement footprint but includes Message Testing, which is a high-value qualitative offering

Pipeline to Watch

These assets will generate new project requests as they advance. Flag them in account planning conversations.

Asset Mechanism Stage Why It Matters
Pasritamig (JNJ-78278343) T-cell redirecting bispecific (KLK2xCD3) for mCRPC Fast Track designated Generating significant hype in GU oncology; expect launch tracking asks
JNJ-79635322 Trispecific antibody Early pipeline Novel modality — may require new tracking frameworks
JNJ-90014496 Bispecific CAR-T (CD19/CD20) Early pipeline Cell therapy entry; different commercial model
Bleximenib Small molecule for KMTAr/NPM1m AML Pipeline New heme indication outside MM
RYBREVANT SubQ Subcutaneous amivantamab Delayed (CRL) Would reshape SFEA messaging narratives when approved
RYBREVANT expansions Head & Neck, Colorectal/GI (OrigAMI) Ongoing New TAs = new competitive landscapes to track
INLEXZO expansions SunRISe-3/4/5 (MIBC, BCG-naive, recurrent) Ongoing Could 3-5x addressable patient population
AKEEGA in mCSPC AMPLITUDE trial Ongoing Would move AKEEGA earlier in treatment, expanding market

ZoomRx's Service Footprint

The matrix below shows the breadth of ZoomRx engagement across J&J's therapeutic areas. Each cell represents an active or recently active project type.

Project Type Lung (NSCLC) GU (Prostate/Bladder) Heme (MM/CLL/WM) Immunology Neuroscience Cardiopulmonary
Ferma Congress X X X X
Sales Rep Tracking (SFEA) X X X X X
OCE Tracking X X
MSL Tracking X X X X X
FRM Tracking X X X X
KAM Tracking X X X
KAM & ABS Tracking X
Commercial Analytics X X
Launch KPI Tracking X
ATU / Patient ATU X X
Patient Experience Tracking X
Digital Tracking X
Bispecifics Pharmacists Tracking X
Qual Interviews X
Sales Simulation X
HCP-Patient Conversations X
Message Testing X

Key takeaway: Heme runs the widest project portfolio (13+ types). Lung and GU are SFEA-heavy. Immunology and Neuroscience are narrower but consistent. New project types tend to pilot in Heme before expanding to other TAs.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Understanding competitive dynamics is essential because they drive the analytical narratives in every readout we deliver.

Brand Primary Competitor Competitive Dynamic Implication for ZoomRx Work
RYBREVANT TAGRISSO (osimertinib, AstraZeneca) MARIPOSA positions RYB+LAZCLUZE as 1L alternative to TAGRISSO in common EGFR; TAGRISSO has massive incumbent share and physician comfort SFEA tracks rep message effectiveness head-to-head; every wave benchmarks RYB reps vs TAG reps on reach, frequency, and message impact
ERLEADA Xtandi (enzalutamide, Pfizer/Astellas); Nubeqa (darolutamide, Bayer) Three-way ARPI competition in nmCRPC/mCSPC; differentiation on tolerability and trial data (SPARTAN/TITAN) SFEA and ATU track share of voice and prescribing intent across all three ARPIs
AKEEGA Lynparza (olaparib, AZ); other PARP combos Biomarker-driven (BRCA+); limited addressable market means every patient decision matters Messaging around biomarker testing and combo benefit vs mono PARP is central to rep tracking
INLEXZO Adstiladrin (nadofaragene, Ferring); Anktiva (nogapendekin alfa, ImmunityBio) Small market, high unmet need; all three are new-to-market in BCG-unresponsive NMIBC Launch KPI and early adoption tracking; competitive narrative is about procedural convenience and durability
DARZALEX Sarclisa (isatuximab, Sanofi) DARZALEX is dominant; Sarclisa competes in specific combo regimens Less about competitive threat, more about line-of-therapy expansion and maintaining backbone status
TECVAYLI / TALVEY CAR-T therapies (Abecma, Carvykti); other bispecifics (Elrexfio) Bispecific vs CAR-T positioning in late-line MM; TALVEY's non-BCMA target is differentiator Bispecifics Pharmacists Tracking and SFEA focus on sequencing decisions and safety narratives
SPRAVATO Generic ketamine clinics; TMS; traditional antidepressants REMS requirement creates access friction; efficacy narrative competes with convenience of alternatives HCP-Patient Conversations project uniquely captures the shared decision-making dynamic
TREMFYA Skyrizi (risankizumab, AbbVie); Stelara (loss of exclusivity) IL-23 class competition; TREMFYA expanding into GI and Rheum from Derm base Multi-TA tracking across Derm/GI/Rheum means competitive set shifts by indication

Bottom line for a new Principal: The RYBREVANT vs TAGRISSO dynamic will consume the most analytical bandwidth. ERLEADA's three-way ARPI race is the second most complex competitive narrative. Everything else is either established dominance (DARZALEX) or early-launch positioning (INLEXZO, bispecifics). Know these competitive stories cold — they are the lens through which every data point gets interpreted in client readouts.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
